project.title: Identification of Vha68-3 binding proteins by LC-MS/MS
project.projectDescription: To explore the regulatory network involving Vha68-3 binding proteins, immunocoprecipitation (IP) experiments were performed in S2 cells via LC-MS/MS analysis and Label-free quantification.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Drosophila Schneider 2 (S2) cells (ATCC, CRL-1963) were cultured at 28°C in Schneider’s Drosophila medium (21720024, Gibco) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (04-001-1ACS, Bioind). HA-Vha68-3 fusion proteins were expressed using the pUAS-attB plasmid. S2 cells were transfected using Effectene Transfection Reagent (301425, Qiagen). Immunoprecipitation was performed by extracting proteins from transfected cells, binding them to HA-tag labeled beads, and eluting for further analysis. The bound protein was eluted by heating the beads at 95 °C for 5 min with 2× SDS buffer and then separated by SDS-PAGE. The gels were cut into 1mm³ particles, and were washed with water, 50% ACN, and 100% ACN. The gel particles were reduced with DTT, alkylated with iodoacetamide and digested with Trypsin at 37°C for 12 hours. After stopping the digestion with TFA, peptides were extracted from the gel particles, then desalted using StageTip (Thermofisher) before mass spectrometric analysis. For MS analysis, the purified peptides were resuspended in 0.1% formic acid (FA), separated on an analytical column (75 μm × 160 mm, 1.9 μm, Dr. Maisch) and analyzed using Orbitrap Fusion Lumos mass spectrometer (Thermofisher) equipped with Easy-nLC 1200 system (Thermofisher). The data-dependent acquisition (DDA) mode was applied while MS1 data were obtained in the Orbitrap with mass range of 350-1500 using 60k resolution, and MS/MS was performed by high energy dissociation (HCD) in the Orbitrap with a resolution of 15k.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The MaxQuant software (version: 1.6.5.0) was used for searching the raw files against Drosophila melanogaster proteome database (downloaded on Jun, 2021). Identified peptides and proteins were filtered at a false discovery rate (FDR) of 1%. Searches were performed using Trypsin/P enzyme specificity while allowing up to two missed cleavages. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine residues (+57.0215Da) were set as fixed modifications. Variable modifications included oxidation of methionines and acetylation of protein N termini.
